chemistry.scope_summary: Photocatalytic degradation of methylene blue dye using ZnO nanorods with different aspect ratios under high-pressure sodium lamp irradiation. Higher aspect ratio ZnO nanorods with surface defects showed enhanced photocatalytic activity, with 99.3% degradation achieved after 80 minutes using the 350°C sample.

optimization_table.key_findings: 350°C sample showed highest photocatalytic activity with 99.3% degradation

translation_logic.batch_limitation: No flow chemistry discussed - this is a batch photocatalysis study

figure_descriptions: XRD patterns of ZnO at different calcination temperatures

notes: This is a batch photocatalysis study, not a flow chemistry paper. The study focuses on optimizing ZnO nanorod morphology through calcination temperature for methylene blue degradation. No flow chemistry aspects are present.
